title: Publishable website package is not marked private
reasoning: This appears to be an Astro website package, not a reusable npm library, but the manifest has a name and version and does not set `private: true`. That leaves accidental `pnpm publish` or automation-triggered publication possible, which can expose package metadata or reserve an unintended npm package name.
reproduction: Run `pnpm publish --dry-run`; npm tooling will treat the manifest as publishable because it is not private.
recommendation: Add `"private": true` near the top of package.json unless this project is intentionally meant to be published as an npm package.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No release or publish-safety tests are linked for this feature.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a lightweight manifest check that asserts private is true for this website project, if the repository has a package metadata validation step.
